[AGRICULTURE–NOTIFICATION OF CROPS, DISTRICTS, FIRKAS AND REVENUE VILLAGES FOR AGRICULTURE 
AND HORTICULTURE CROPS FOR RABI SEASON 2019-2020 UNDER PRADHAN MANTRI FASAL BIMA YOJANA 
ORDERS- ISSUED.]
The following Government Order is published:—
[G.O. (D)No. 256, Agriculture (AP2), 19th November 2019, è£˜ˆF¬è 3,

*****
ORDER: No. 256, Agriculture (AP2), 19th November 2019.
In the Government order first read above, orders have been issued according administrative approval for 
the implementation of Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY) during 2019-2020 in Tamil Nadu at a cost of 
Rs. 634.7438 crore in all the districts of Tamil Nadu (except Chennai).
2. The 45th State Level Coordination Committee on Crop Insurance meeting for notifying Rabi Crops during
2019-2020 under Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY) was held on 6.11.2019 and the Committee accepted 
to notify the Crops, Districts, Firkas and Revenue Villages for Agricultural and Horticultural Crops, for notification 
of Insurance Units and publication in the State Gazette.


2
3. In his letter third read above, the Director of Agriculture, based on recommendation of the 45th State 
level Co-ordination Committee on Crop lnsurance meeting to notify crops during Rabi season, 2019-2020 held on 
06.11.2019 under the Chairmanship of Agricultural Production Commissioner and Principal Secretary to Government, 
Agriculture Department has sent the proposal for notification of Crops, Districts, Firkas, Revenue Villages, Insurance 
Units and Approved Premium Rates for Agriculture and Horticulture Crops during Rabi, 2019-2020 
4. The Government, after careful examination of the proposal of the Director of Agriculture, accept the same 
and accordingly notify the Crops, Districts, Firkas, Revenue Villages, insurance units and approved premium rates 
for Agricultural and Horticultural Crops for the effective implementation of the Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana 
during Rabi, 2019-2020 season as detailed in the Annexures I, II, Ill, IV and V to this order.
5. The claims under the Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana Scheme shall be settled on the basis of yield 
data furnished by the Department of Economics and Statistics as per prescribed cut-off dates for the notified 
crops / districts / firkas / revenue villages for the effective implementation of Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana 
scheme during Rabi 2019-2020 season and not on the basis of the Annavari Certificate / Gazette notification 
declaring the area as Drought / Flood / Cyclone affected etc., by the District Collectors.
(By Order of the Governor)
	
	
GAGANDEEP SINGH BEDI, 
	
	
Agricultural Production Commissioner and
	
	
Principal Secretary to Government.
PRADHAN MANTRI FASAL BIMA YOJANA (PMFBY) NOTIFICATION FOR RABI SEASON 2019-2020
ANNEXURE I
[G.O.(D).No. 256, Agriculture (AP2) Department, Dated: 19.11.2019]
Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY) will be implemented in all the districts of Tamil, Nadu (except 
Chennai) during Rabi, 2019-2020 season as per the Revised Operational Guidelines of Government of India 
(Government of India Letter No.13015/03/2016-Credit II, Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare, Department of 
Agriculture, Co-operation & Farmers Welfare, Krishi Bhavan, New Delhi, dated 28.09.2018).
I. OBJECTIVES OF PRADHAN MANTRI FASAL BIMA YOJANA:
Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana aims at supporting sustainable production in agriculture by way of-
a)	
Providing financial support to farmers suffering crop loss/damage arising out of unforeseen events.
b)	
Stabilizing the income of farmers to ensure their continuance in farming.
c)	
Encouraging farmers to adopt innovative and modern Agriculture practices.
d)	
Ensuring flow of credit to the agriculture sector, which will, contribute to food security, crop diversification 
	
	
and enhancing growth and competitiveness of agriculture sector besides protecting farmers from production 
	
	
risks.
II. COVERAGE OF FARMERS:
All farmers including share croppers and tenant farmers growing the notified crops in the notified areas are 
eligible for coverage.
•	
Compulsory for Loanee farmers.
•	
Optional for Non–Loanee farmers.
Ill. COVERAGE OF RISKS AND EXCLUSIONS:
The following stages of the crop and Risks leading to crop loss are covered under the scheme:
1.	 PREVENTED SOWING / PLANTING / GERMINATION RISK- If Insured area is prevented from 
sowing / planting / germination due to deficit rainfall or adverse seasonal weather conditions.
2.	 STANDING CROP (SOWING TO HARVESTING)–Comprehensive risk insurance is provided to 
cover yield losses due to non–preventable risks viz. Drought, dry spells, flood, inundation, wide spread pests 
and diseases, landslides, fire due to natural causes, lightening, storm, hailstorm and cyclone.


3
3. 	 LOCALIZED CALAMITIES–Loss/damage to notified insured crops resulting from occurrence ot 
identified localized risks of hailstorm, landslide, inundation (except Paddy, Sugarcane), cloudburst and natural 
fire due to lightening affecting isolated farms in the notified area.
4. 	 ON ACCOUNT PAYMENT OF CLAIMS DUE TO MID-SEASON ADVERSITY- Insurance cover 
will be provided to the farmers to provide immediate relief in case of adverse seasonal conditions viz. 
floods, prolonged dry spells, severe drought etc., during the crop season wherein expected yield during the 
season is likely to be less than 50% of Normal Yield.
5. 	 POST-HARVEST LOSSES–Coverage is available only upto a maximum period of two weeks from 
harvesting, for those crops which are required to be dried in cut and spread / small bundled condition 
in the field after harvesting against specific perils of hailstorm, cyclone, cyclonic rains and unseasonal rains.
IV. NOTIFICATION OF IMPLEMENTING AGENCY
The following Insurance Companies are notified to implement Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana during 
Rabi, 2019-2020 Season in all the districts except Chennai as per the tender agreement. (Work order issued to 
the Insurance Companies vide-Director of Agriculture Letter No.G1/126094/2018-1, dated 4.3.2019 and Contract 
Agreement signed between Department of Agriculture & the Insurance Companies).
Clusters
Approved Implementing Agency
I, II, Ill & V
Agriculture Insurance Company of India Limited.
IV & VI
Oriental Insurance Company Limited.
V. NOTIFICATION OF CLUSTER & INDEMNITY LEVEL
The following six clusters and also the Indemnity Level indicated thereon for the purpose of calculation of 
Threshold yield are notified. The Indemnity Level for the purpose of calculation of Threshold yield is fixed at 90% 
for low risk districts, 80% for medium risk districts and 70% for high risk districts.

VI.  DISTRICTS AND INSURANCE  UNITS  NOTIFIED  FOR  RABI  OTHER  CROPS  DURING  2019-2020 
      UNDER  PRADHAN MANTRI FASAL BIMA YOJANA
As approved in the 45th	
State Level Coordination Committee on Crop Insurance meeting held on 06.11.2019, 
Revenue Village is notified as insurance unit for major crops and Firka for minor crops. during Rabi, 2019–2020 
Season under  PMFBY (District wise, Crop wise list of units furnished in Annexure-II).
The following additional units are notified as per Para 7.2.7 of Operational Guidelines with the consent of 
Agriculture Insurance Company of India Limited & Oriental Insurance Company Limited:-
a.	
Agriculture Crops :
i.	
13 villages in 12 additional clusters & 1 village in the existing Notified Unit in Erode & Tiruchirappalli 
	
where Oriental Insurance Company Limited is the Implementing Agency.
ii.	 87 villages in 22 additional clusters & 12 villages in the existing Notified Units of Thanjavur, Salem, 
	
Villupuram & Madurai where Agriculture Insurance Company of India Limited is the implementing 
	
Agency.	
b.   Horticulture Crops:
i.	
Additional 5 Notified units (5 Revenue Villages) for Garlic and 3 Notified Units for Cabbage (8 Revenue 
	
Villages) in the Nilgiris District and 6 Notified units (11 revenue villages) for Banana crop in Thanjavur 
	
district where Agriculture Insurance Company of India Limited is the implementing agency.
ii.	
Additional 6 Notified units (19 revenue villages) for Coriander crop in Ramanathapuram district where 
	
Oriental Insurance Company Limited is the implementing agency.

The Insurance Companies are also permitted to reconcile with the Department of Economics & Statistics 
(DES) for any discrepancies noted in the hierarchy of the notified units at any stage of implementation of the 
scheme during enrolment, implementation I uploading in Government portal while processing the compensation.
VII. SUM INSURED:
The Scale of Finance approved by State Level Technical Committee for the year 2019-2020 and notified vide 
G.O.(Ms).No.78, Agriculture (AP2) department, dated 11.04.2019 should be adopted as Sum Insured for the crops 
notified during Rabi, 2019-2020 season as per Para No.12.1 of the Revised Operational Guidelines of Pradhan 
Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana .The Sum Insured will be same for both loanee and non-loanee farmers
VIII. CLUSTER-WISE, DISTRICT-WISE APPROVED PREMIUM RATE AND THE PREMIUM RATE 
FOR FARMERS FOR RABI, 2019-2020 SEASON
As approved in the tender agreement, the crop-wise, cluster-wise, district-wise approved premium rate and 
the premium rate for farmers are notified as per Annexure Ill for Rabi, 2019-2020 Season (rate of Insurance 
Charges payable by the farmer is 1.5% of Sum Insured or Actuarial rate, whichever is less for all food grain 
(Cereals, Millets & Pulses) & Oilseed crops and 5% of sum insured or actuarial rate whichever is less for Annual 
Commercial I Annual Horticultural crops and Perennial Horticultural Crops).
IX.	 NOTIFICATION OF THRESHOLD YIELD FOR THE NOTIFIED UNITS IN THE HIERARCHY AS PER 
NOTIFICATION
The Insurance Companies have to calculate and upload the threshold yield at Insurance unit level on the 
National Crop Insurance Portal. The Insurance Companies shall also furnish the Threshold yield to Government 
after receipt of Crop Cutting Experiment Yield data from Department of Economics and Statistics.
X.	 FURNISHING OF AREA SOWN DETAILS WITHIN TWO MONTHS FROM THE SOWING SEASON AND 
G-RETURN TWICE A YEAR
The Department of Economics & Statistics shall ensure that the reconciled (District Revenue Officer Report) 
Insurance Unit wise monthly area sown details which are subjected to change are furnished temporarily to the 
Insurance Companies by the District Deputy Director of Statistics and the final area sown details is furnished 
before 29th May, 2020 for Rabi season.
XI.  SEASONALITY DISCIPLINE / CUT-OFF DATE FOR RABI, 2019-2020
The cluster-wise, district-wise crop-wise seasonality discipline (Annexure IV) is notified. It is also permitted to 
treat the next working day as the cut-off date in case if the cut-off date falls on a public holiday or is declared 
as public holiday by the Government or there is disruption of services due to strikes/shut-down etc as per the 
Para 16.3 of Operational Guidelines.


7
XII.	 	 SEASONALITY FOR FURNISHING CROP CUTTING EXPERIMENT FORM I & II AND ACTUAL YIELD 
DATA FOR RABI, 2019-2020
The cluster-wise, district-wise crop-wise seasonality discipline for furnishing Crop Cutting Experiment 
Form I & II and actual yield data by the Department of Economics and Statistics (Annexure V) are notified and 
permitted to adopt Para 16.3 of Operational Guidelines.
XIII.	PRIORITISING THE ADOPTION OF YIELD OF NEIGHBOURING INSURANCE UNIT WHEN 
ADEQUATE CROP CUTTING EXPERIMENTS COULD NOT BE CONDUCTED
	The Priority of applicability is notified as stated below:
	•	
Priority 1 : Adopting the yield of a neighbouring Insurance Unit with maximum correlation
	•	
Priority 2 : Adopting yield estimate of next higher unit
The decision of State Level Technical Advisory Committee (STAC) will be final in case of any disputes 
regarding adoption of yield of a neighbouring Insurance Unit.
XIV.	SEASONALITY DISCIPLINE FOR PREVENTED SOWING / GERMINATION RISK
The District Level Monitoring Committee under the Chairmanship of District Collector is authorized to fix the 
seasonality discipline for prevented sowing I failed sowing by adopting the conditions formulated in the Revised 
Operational Guidelines. The Insurance Companies are also directed to make an appeal to invoke prevented sowing 
I germination risk only within prescribed period with proper evidence (i.e.) not later than 15 days from cut off 
date for enrolment of farmer for prevented sowing (Para 21.3.2.4 of Operational Guidelines) and not later than 
3 weeks after peak sowing period in case of germination risk (G.O.(Ms).No.293, Agriculture (AP6) Department, 
dated 12.09.2016). The Insurance Companies shall also not make an appeal at any date later than the timeline 
fixed or after the receipt of yield data from the Department of Economics and Statistics.
In case, this provision is not invoked by the District Level Monitoring Committee I District Level Joint Committee, 
Insurance Company may escalate the same in writing to State Government within the stipulated timeline (Para 
21.3.2.5. of Operational Guidelines). Insurance Companies shall use products viz., Satellite based Vegetation 
Index, Weather parameters, etc. or other technologies (including statistical test, crop models etc.) to decide about 
invoking of prevented sowing I germination risk within the stipulated timeline (as per Para 21.3.6.1.3 of Operational 
Guidelines) or to confirm the yield estimates when the yield estimates reported at IU level are abnormally low or 
high vis-a-vis the general crop condition (as per Para 19.2 of Operational Guidelines).
The Insurance Companies are also directed-
a.	 to inform  Agricultural  Production  Commissioner  & Principal Secretary to Government, Agriculture 
Department  / Director of Agriculture / Director of Horticulture &Plantation Crops if area sown details 
are not furnished in time.
b.	 to disburse the claim for prevented sowing / planting / germination risk within 30 days of the State’s order / 
notification invoking the. event of the insured risk, subject to the data on estimated area sown having been 
received from State Government (Para 21.3.5.4. of Operational Guidelines).
c.	 to disburse the pay-out under the cover without waiting for the receipt of final Government share 
of premium subsidy. (Para 21.3.5.5. of Operational Guidelines).
The Financial institutions are also directed to-
a.	 ensure that they debit farmers’ premium within 15 days from sanction/renewal of KCC/crop loan, failing 
which banks will be liable to meet the claim liabilities of uncovered eligible farmers. (Para21.3.2.3. of Operational 
Guidelines).
b.	 remit farmers’ premium to Insurance Company, with loanee farmers list, within 7 days of intimation of loss 
under this section, if not uploaded earlier on Portal. However, this option must be exercised 
as last recourse with proper justification. (Para 21.3.5.9 of Operational Guidelines).
The Tamil Nadu Agricultural University is also directed-
	
a. to provide Remote sensing based information and Crop Signatures on payment within 15 days period from 
	
the date of receipt of request by the notified Insurance Companies


8
XV	
NOTIFICATION OF DISTRICTS FOR LOCALIZED PERILS, MID-SEASON ADVERSITIES AND POST 
HARVEST LOSSES
	
The following districts are notified for localized perils, mid-season adversity and post harvest losses as indicated

XVI.	 APPLICATION OF REMOTE SENSING BASED INFORMATION & INSURANCE FOR CROPS IN 
EMERGING ECONOMIES (RIICE)
The Remote Sensing Based Information & Insurance for Crops in Emerging Economies (RIICE) Project of 
Tamil Nadu Agricultural University (TNAU) is notified for Computation of Area under Prevented Sowing for Paddy, 
Maize and Cotton during the 3 year contract period from 2019-2020 to 2021-2022.  The Tamil Nadu Agricultural 
University is also directed to abide by G.O.(Ms) No.293, Agriculture (AP-6) Department, dated 12.09.2016 with 
regard to prevented / failed sowing risks and provide Remote sensing based information and Crop Signatures on 
payment within 15 days period from the date of receipt of request by the selected Insurance Companies.
XVII.   DIGITIZATION OF LAND RECORDS
The Insurance Companies are directed to accept the e-adangal / Sowing certificate generated through 
e-adangal web service system as one of the documents to be enclosed with the proposal for enrolment once 
the process of issuance is finalized.
XVIII.	
APPLICATION OF AREA CORRECTION FACTOR (ACF) :
	
The Insurance Companies are directed-
	
•	
to avoid application of Area Correction Factor if the difference between area insured and area sown is 
	
	
less than or equal to 30% at Block/ Tehsill Taluk. 
	
•	
to access to the final Area sown details provided by the Department of Economics & Statistics at the 
	
	
time of calculation of compensation claims.	 In case of differences more than 30% the Insurance 
	
	
Companies to abide para 25.3 of operational guidelines in referring to Mahalanobis National Crop 
	
	
Forecast Centre.
	
•	
to upload the data on application of Area Correction Factor on National Crop Insurance Portal.
XIX.  REMITTANCE OF FARMERS’ PREMIUM
The Government directs the
	
 —	 bankers to ensure that the premium for both loanee and non-loanee farmers is remitted to concerned 
	
	
Insurance Companies and the related data uploaded on National Crop Insurance Portal within the 
	
	
prescribed time.
	
	
	
o	
Concerned Bank and its branches / CSCs shall be made liable to pay admissible claims 
	
	
	
	
to farmers if they fail to follow the procedure delineated in the Operational Guidelines.
	
—	
Insurance Companies to verify the applications, check the premium amount received, if necessary, get the 
	
	
proposals corrected by Financial Institutions / Common Service Centres, accept the corrected proposals 
	
	
and process the proposals within the timeline stipulated.


9
	
—	
Financial Institutions / ICs not to return back the premium to the farmers at any date later to the timeframe 
	
	
mentioned in the Guidelines.
	
—	
State Level Bankers Committee (SLBC), Registrar of Co-operative Societies (RCS) & Insurance Companies 
	
	
to
	
	
	
o	
furnish consolidated debit and remittance report of farmers’ premium every month to Government 
	
	
	
	
for review by Agricultural Production Commissioner & Principal Secretary to Government, 
	
	
	
	
Agriculture Department.
	
	
	
o	
upload the details of remittance on the software developed by Department of Agriculture on 
	
	
	
	
28th of every month.
XX. COMBATING ENROLMENT MALPRACTICES UNDER PMFBY
The Government permits the stakeholders to display the names of the fraudulent farmers on the notice boards 
of enrolment centers besides debarring them from the Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana scheme for atleast 
6 seasons (2 years) to manage the risk of fraud. The Government also directs the Director of Agriculture / 
Director of Horticulture & Plantation Crops to address the District Collectors and Financial Institutions to debar the 
farmers from enrolling under Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana and not to collect premium from such farmers.
XXI. FORM OF FINAL PRODUCE TO BE ADOPTED FOR COMPUTATION OF COMPENSATION CLAIMS:
The following Yield unit provided by the Department of Economics & Statistics (already furnished in the 
Tender) for Rabi Crops notified during 2019-2020 should be taken for computation of Threshold Yield and in 
terms of Actual Yield for calculating the compensation claims:-
Crop
Form of Final Produce
Agriculture Crops
Paddy
Rice (Kg/Hec)
Maize, Cholam, Cumbu & Ragi
Dry grains (Kg/Hec)
Redgram, Blackgram, Rice fallow 
Blackgram, Greengram, Rice 
fallow Greengram, Bengalgram, 
Horse gram & Cowpea
Dry grains (Kg/Hec)
Sunflower, Gingelly
Dry seeds (Kg/Hec)
Ground nut
Dry pods (Kg/Hec)
Cotton, Rice Fallow Cotton
Lint (Kg/Hec)
Sugarcane
Sugarcane (Tonnes/Hec)
Horticulture Crops
Banana
Fresh Fruit (Kg/Hec)
Onion
Bulb (Kg/Hec)
Chillies
Dry pods (Kg/Hec)
Turmeric
Dry Rhizome (Kg/Hec)
Tapioca
Fresh tuber(Kg/Hec)
Potato
Fresh tuber (Kg/Hec)
Garlic
Bulb (Kg/Hec)
Ginger
Wet rhizome (Kg/Hec)
Carrot
Fresh tuber (Kg/Hec)
Bhendi
Fresh fruit (Kg/Hec)
Brinjal
Fresh fruit (Kg/Hec)
Cabbage
Fresh Head (Kg/Hec)
Mango
Fresh fruit (Kg/Hec)
Tomato
Fresh fruit (Kg/Hec)
Coriander
Dry seeds(Kg/Hec)
Grapes
Fresh fruit (Kg/Hec)


10
XXII. MANDATORY DOCUMENTS FOR ENROLMENT
	
	
The following documents are mandatory for enrolment
	
	
•	
Proposal form
	
	
•	
Registration form
	
	
•	
Adangal
	
	
	
o	
Adangal alone for enrolment of Loanee & Non-Loanee farmers in normal cases.
	
	
	
o	
Sowing certificate issued by VAO in case of prevented sowing and purchase of insurance 
	
	
	
	
before sowing
	
	
•	
A copy of Aadhar Card
	
	
•	
A copy of first page of Bank Pass Book
